title: Aluminum nitride substrate removal for ultraviolet light-emitting devices
abstract: In various embodiments, extraction efficiency of light-emitting devices fabricated on aluminum nitride substrates is enhanced via removal of at least a portion of the substrate.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of forming an illumination device, the method comprising: forming a release layer over an aluminum nitride substrate, the release layer having an absorption band corresponding to a first wavelength of light; forming an active light-emitting device structure over the release layer; and exposing the release layer to light having the first wavelength, whereby absorption of the light by the release layer results in separation of at least a portion of the substrate from the active light-emitting device structure. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein forming the release layer comprises depositing aluminum nitride and intentionally introducing one or more dopants into at least a portion of the aluminum nitride. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the one or more dopants comprise oxygen. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the aluminum nitride substrate is single- crystalline. 5. The method of claim 1, further comprising, before exposing the release layer to light, attaching the active light-emitting device structure to a handle wafer. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the release layer is exposed to light through the aluminum nitride substrate. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the aluminum nitride substrate is substantially transparent to light having the first wavelength. 8. The method of claim 1, further comprising applying heat to the aluminum nitride substrate. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the release layer is substantially lattice- matched to the aluminum nitride substrate. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the release layer has a thermal conductivity lower than a thermal conductivity of the aluminum nitride substrate. 11. A method of forming an illumination device, the method comprising: providing an aluminum nitride substrate having a patterned top surface defining a plurality of protruding pillars; depositing an active light-emitting device structure over the patterned top surface of the aluminum nitride substrate, whereby enclosed voids are formed at the interface between the aluminum substrate and the active light-emitting device structure via lateral growth of at least a portion of the active light-emitting device structure; and applying radiation proximate the voids, whereby heating of the voids and/or material therebetween by the radiation results in separation of at least a portion of the substrate from the active light-emitting device structure. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein: the active light-emitting device structure comprises, proximate the interface between the aluminum substrate and the active light-emitting device structure, a release layer having an absorption band corresponding to a first wavelength of light; and at least a portion of the radiation applied to the voids comprises light of the first wavelength. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising forming the release layer at least in part by depositing aluminum nitride and intentionally introducing one or more dopants into at least a portion of the aluminum nitride. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the one or more dopants comprise oxygen. 15. The method of claim 12, wherein the release layer is substantially lattice- matched to the aluminum nitride substrate. 16. The method of claim 12, wherein the release layer has a thermal conductivity lower than a thermal conductivity of the aluminum nitride substrate. 17. The method of claim 11, wherein the aluminum nitride substrate is single- crystalline. 18. The method of claim 11, further comprising, before applying the radiation, attaching the active light-emitting device structure to a handle wafer. 19. The method of claim 11, wherein the voids are exposed to radiation through the aluminum nitride substrate. 20. A method of forming an illumination device, the method comprising: providing an aluminum nitride substrate having a patterned top surface defining a plurality of pillars protruding therefrom; depositing an active light-emitting device structure over the patterned top surface of the aluminum nitride substrate, whereby enclosed voids are formed at the interface between the aluminum substrate and the active light-emitting device structure via lateral growth of at least a portion of the active light-emitting device structure; and removing a first portion of the aluminum nitride substrate to thereby open the voids, additional portions of the aluminum nitride substrate remaining disposed on the active light-emitting device structure between the opened voids. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the first portion of the aluminum nitride substrate is removed by etching, grinding, and/or polishing. 22. The method of claim 20, further comprising shaping at least one of the additional portions of the aluminum nitride substrate, whereby a shape of the at least one shaped additional portion enhances photon extraction from the active light- emitting device structure. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the at least one of the additional portions is shaped by non-conformal etching. 24. An illumination device comprising: a first contact layer having a first polarity; a second contact layer having a second polarity opposite the first polarity, at least a portion of the second contact layer defining a plurality of pillars separated by recesses disposed therebetween; a light-emitting structure disposed between the first and second contact layers, the light-emitting structure comprising a multiple-quantum well layer comprising a plurality of periods each comprising a strained Al x Gai- x N barrier and a strained AlyGai-yN quantum well, x and y being different by an amount facilitating confinement of charge carriers in the multiple-quantum well layer, and y being selected for emission of ultraviolet light by the light-emitting structure; and a single-crystalline aluminum nitride cap disposed on a top surface of each of the pillars defined by the second contact layer. 25. The device of claim 24, wherein each of the aluminum nitride caps has a curved top surface. 26. The device of claim 24, wherein each of the aluminum nitride caps is substantially hemispherical. 27. The device of claim 24, wherein each of the aluminum nitride caps is conical or pyramidal. 28. The device of claim 24, further comprising: a first electrical contact disposed in contact with the first contact layer; and a second electrical contact disposed in contact with the second contact layer. 29. The device of claim 24, further comprising a handle wafer affixed to the first contact layer. 30. The device of claim 29, wherein the handle wafer comprises at least one of glass, sapphire, or quartz.
